---
pagetitle: "Tool Registry - Architecture 2.0"
description: "A maintainer-reviewed registry of simulators, proxy models, verification harnesses, benchmarks, datasets, and data representations for auditable computer-architecture and computing-system design loops."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
listing:
  - id: tool-catalog
    contents: tools.yml
    template: _tool-card.ejs.md
    categories: true
    filter-ui: true
    page-size: 100
    sort-ui: false
    fields: [title, description, artifact_availability, last_verified, tags, authors, institution, submitted_by, categories]
    field-required: [title, url, description, artifact_availability, categories]
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">Living registry</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Tool registry</div>
  <h1>Tool registry</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">The action space for AI-assisted architecture</p>
</section>

<p class="site-lede">
The tools below make up the working environment an architecture design loop
operates in: the simulators and proxy models that supply feedback, the
verification harnesses, benchmarks, and datasets that supply evidence, and the
data representations that let a loop read and write a design. Each entry should
make its role in the loop visible, so the registry stays an architecture agenda
rather than a general list of interesting projects.
</p>

<p class="site-lede">
The registry is maintainer-reviewed. If you have built an open-source simulator,
surrogate model, verification harness, or agentic workflow, you can
<a href="/submit.html">propose
it for the registry</a>. Submissions include authors, institutions, papers,
artifact status, tags, and a short fit note for maintainers. The public card
keeps the visible information compact: summary, credit, category, and tags.
</p>

<div class="registry-legend" aria-label="Registry governance">
  <strong>Registry governance</strong>
  <span><b>Owner:</b> Architecture 2.0 repository maintainers.</span>
  <span><b>Inclusion:</b> fixed resource category, primary URL, concise identity, architecture-study fit, and maintainer review.</span>
  <span><b>Updated:</b> each card carries its own verification date; the release snapshot was frozen July 14, 2026.</span>
  <span><b>Retirement:</b> broken, superseded, misleading, or out-of-scope entries are corrected or removed through review; repository history preserves prior states.</span>
  <span><b>Archive:</b> <a href="snapshots/registry-2026-07-14.yml">frozen YAML for this print release</a>.</span>
</div>

<div class="registry-legend" aria-label="Artifact availability definitions">
  <strong>Availability labels</strong>
  <span><b>Runnable</b> means a maintainer executed a documented example.</span>
  <span><b>Source available</b> means source or project materials are linked, but execution was not independently checked.</span>
  <span><b>Benchmark available</b> and <b>Dataset available</b> identify public benchmark or data artifacts.</span>
  <span><b>Paper only</b> means the registry has a publication but no public implementation link.</span>
  <span>The verification date records the primary-link check, not a claim that the artifact runs.</span>
</div>

<nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Tool registry actions">
  <a class="site-action" href="/submit.html">Submit a tool <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span></a>
  <a class="site-action secondary" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/discussions">Discuss fit</a>
  <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/workshops.html">See workshops</a>
</nav>

<div class="category-strip" role="group" aria-label="Tool registry categories">
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('')">All</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('U2ltdWxhdGlvbg==')">Simulation</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('UHJveHklMjBNb2RlbHM=')">Proxy models</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('QWdlbnRpYyUyMFdvcmtmbG93cw==')">Agentic workflows</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('RGF0YSUyMFJlcHJlc2VudGF0aW9ucw==')">Data representations</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('VmVyaWZpY2F0aW9u')">Verification</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('QmVuY2htYXJrcyUyMGFuZCUyMERhdGFzZXRz')">Benchmarks and datasets</button>
  <button type="button" onclick="window.quartoListingCategory('UGh5c2ljYWwlMjBEZXNpZ24=')">Physical design</button>
</div>
</div>
```

:::{#tool-catalog}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
  var listing = window.quartoListings?.["listing-tool-catalog"] ||
    window["quarto-listings"]?.["listing-tool-catalog"];
  var input = document.querySelector("#listing-tool-catalog .search");
  if (!listing || !input) {
    return;
  }
  input.addEventListener("input", function () {
    listing.search(input.value);
  });
});
</script>
```
